clusters.title: US hits dozens of countries with new wave of tariffs
clusters.neutral_headline: US hits dozens of countries with new wave of tariffs
bullet_summary: The White House said the duties target countries that have failed to curb imports made with forced labour
Reported by BBC World, NPR News, France24 and others

items.title: US hits dozens of countries with new wave of tariffs
items.standfirst: Imposed citing forced labour concerns, the levies replace a temporary global duty brought in after the US Supreme Court struck down tariffs in February.
items.content: Imposed citing forced labour concerns, the levies replace a temporary global duty brought in after the US Supreme Court struck down tariffs in February.

items.title: Morning news brief
items.standfirst: U.S.-Iran strikes escalate, affecting global energy markets, Trump imposes double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries, Vigil held in Madison, Wisconsin, for man killed by police.
items.content: U.S.-Iran strikes escalate, affecting global energy markets, Trump imposes double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries, Vigil held in Madison, Wisconsin, for man killed by police.

items.title: US hits partners with new tariffs over forcer labour claims
items.standfirst: Starting this Friday, the United States will impose new tariffs on imports from 60 trading partners, covering 99 percent of all goods entering the country. The new measures replace the temporary 10 percent global tariff introduced 150 days ago after the Supreme Court struck down President Donald Trump's previous sweeping tariff plan. The administration says the latest tariffs are aimed at addressing concerns over goods produced with forced labour. Peter O'Brien reports.
items.content: Starting this Friday, the United States will impose new tariffs on imports from 60 trading partners, covering 99 percent of all goods entering the country. The new measures replace the temporary 10 percent global tariff introduced 150 days ago after the Supreme Court struck down President Donald Trump's previous sweeping tariff plan. The administration says the latest tariffs are aimed at addressing concerns over goods produced with forced labour. Peter O'Brien reports.

items.title: US hits 60 countries with new duties as Trump rebuilds tariff wall
items.standfirst: Washington moves to reimpose 10 per cent duties after previous blanket levies were struck down by Supreme Court
items.content: Washington moves to reimpose 10 per cent duties after previous blanket levies were struck down by Supreme Court

items.title: Trump imposes new tariffs on dozens of countries, citing forced labor concerns
items.standfirst: The Trump administration will impose tariffs of up to 12.5% on goods from 60 U.S. trading partners accused of failing to crack down on forced labor, extending the administration's tariffs on much of the world.
items.content: The Trump administration will impose tariffs of up to 12.5% on goods from 60 U.S. trading partners accused of failing to crack down on forced labor, extending the administration's tariffs on much of the world.

items.title: What to know about Trump’s new tariffs on more than 80 countries
items.standfirst: Tariffs ranging from 10%-12.5%, taking effect on Friday, are US president’s latest effort to circumvent CongressJust before a temporary levy on global imports was set to expire, the Trump administration issued a fresh wave of tariffs on more than 80 countries late on Thursday, ranging from 10% to 12.5%. The new tariffs take effect Friday morning.It’s Donald Trump’s latest effort to continue imposing global levies without congressional approval, despite the supreme court ruling in February that found the US president had illegally used his executive power to implement his global trade policies. Trump has long maintained that the US has been paying more than its fair share in tariffs, and that increased duties are necessary to boost American manufacturing and jobs. Continue reading...
items.content: Tariffs ranging from 10%-12.5%, taking effect on Friday, are US president’s latest effort to circumvent CongressJust before a temporary levy on global imports was set to expire, the Trump administration issued a fresh wave of tariffs on more than 80 countries late on Thursday, ranging from 10% to 12.5%. The new tariffs take effect Friday morning.It’s Donald Trump’s latest effort to continue imposing global levies without congressional approval, despite the supreme court ruling in February that found the US president had illegally used his executive power to implement his global trade policies. Trump has long maintained that the US has been paying more than its fair share in tariffs, and that increased duties are necessary to boost American manufacturing and jobs. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump imposes new double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries
items.standfirst: With existing 10 percent levies set to expire, Trump issues new tariffs on 60 countries under forced labour provision.
items.content: With existing 10 percent levies set to expire, Trump issues new tariffs on 60 countries under forced labour provision.

items.title: Trump imposes new tariffs on dozens of countries over forced labor concerns
items.standfirst: President Trump is imposing new tariffs on dozens of countries accused of failing to crack down on forced labor. CBS News politics reporter Kathryn Watson has the latest.
items.content: President Trump is imposing new tariffs on dozens of countries accused of failing to crack down on forced labor. CBS News politics reporter Kathryn Watson has the latest.

items.title: US imposes new tariffs on 60 countries - including UK
items.standfirst: The Trump administration has imposed new tariffs of at least 10% on imports from 60 countries over claims they failed to prevent forced labour.
items.content: The Trump administration has imposed new tariffs of at least 10% on imports from 60 countries over claims they failed to prevent forced labour.

items.title: Trump to impose double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries
items.standfirst: The United States will slap taxes of 10% to 12.5% on imports from 60 countries accounting for 99% of U.S. imports, charging that they have inadequately enforced bans on goods produced by forced labor.
items.content: The United States will slap taxes of 10% to 12.5% on imports from 60 countries accounting for 99% of U.S. imports, charging that they have inadequately enforced bans on goods produced by forced labor.(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)

items.title: Trump announces double digit tariffs on 60 countries over forced labour concerns
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump imposed new tariffs of up to 12.5% on imports from 60 countries on Thursday, replacing temporary levies that expire on Friday after the Supreme Court struck down his earlier trade measures. The White House said the duties target countries that have failed to curb imports made with forced labour.
items.content: US President Donald Trump imposed new tariffs of up to 12.5% on imports from 60 countries on Thursday, replacing temporary levies that expire on Friday after the Supreme Court struck down his earlier trade measures. The White House said the duties target countries that have failed to curb imports made with forced labour.

clusters.title: What are the Abraham Accords?
bullet_summary: US President Donald Trump said Thursday that a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia depends on the kingdom recognizing Israel -- throwing the agreement into turmoil just the day after it was signed
Reported by Al Jazeera, France24, BBC World and others

items.title: What are the Abraham Accords?
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump has seemingly backpedalled on a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia.
items.content: US President Donald Trump has seemingly backpedalled on a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia.

items.title: Trump stuns Saudis by pinning nuclear deal to Israel ties
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump said Thursday that a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia depends on the kingdom recognizing Israel -- throwing the agreement into turmoil just the day after it was signed. Riyadh has long resisted joining the so-called Abraham Accords with Israel, insisting especially after the Gaza conflict on a pathway to a Palestinian state. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.
items.content: US President Donald Trump said Thursday that a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia depends on the kingdom recognizing Israel -- throwing the agreement into turmoil just the day after it was signed. Riyadh has long resisted joining the so-called Abraham Accords with Israel, insisting especially after the Gaza conflict on a pathway to a Palestinian state. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.

items.title: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
items.standfirst: The US president says the landmark agreement to help Saudis develop nuclear energy is dependent on it joining the Abraham Accords.
items.content: The US president says the landmark agreement to help Saudis develop nuclear energy is dependent on it joining the Abraham Accords.

items.title: Trump’s dubious nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
items.standfirst: An accord lacking ‘gold standard’ safeguards on enrichment would carry proliferation risks
items.content: An accord lacking ‘gold standard’ safeguards on enrichment would carry proliferation risks

items.title: Trump says Saudi nuclear deal contingent on Riyadh normalising relations with Israel
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump on Thursday set a new condition to a US-Saudi nuclear deal signed the previous day by insisting the agreement was "totally subject to" the Gulf kingdom joining the Abraham Accords. Riyadh has resisted normalising relations with Israel, especially after the Gaza war, and the new condition throws the US-Saudi deal into turmoil.
items.content: President Donald Trump on Thursday set a new condition to a US-Saudi nuclear deal signed the previous day by insisting the agreement was "totally subject to" the Gulf kingdom joining the Abraham Accords. Riyadh has resisted normalising relations with Israel, especially after the Gaza war, and the new condition throws the US-Saudi deal into turmoil.

items.title: Trump says Saudi Arabia must normalize Israel ties before nuclear deal is approved
items.standfirst: The president added that the deal “is totally subject to Saudi Arabia joining the very respected and successful Abraham Accords.”
items.content: The president added that the deal “is totally subject to Saudi Arabia joining the very respected and successful Abraham Accords.”

items.title: US signs nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
items.standfirst: The US and Saudi Arabia have reached a nuclear cooperation deal.
items.content: The US and Saudi Arabia have reached a nuclear cooperation deal.

items.title: Major fire after Ukrainian strike on St Petersburg warehouse
items.standfirst: A major fire broke out at a Wildberries e-commerce warehouse in St Petersburg after a Ukrainian drone attack.
items.content: A major fire broke out at a Wildberries e-commerce warehouse in St Petersburg after a Ukrainian drone attack.

items.title: Wildberries says two warehouses were hit near St. Petersburg in a drone attack
items.standfirst: Two warehouses belonging to leading Russian online retailer Wildberries were hit in St. Petersburg and in the Leningrad region, the company said.
items.content: Two warehouses belonging to leading Russian online retailer Wildberries were hit in St. Petersburg and in the Leningrad region, the company said.

items.title: Russia’s biggest online retailer becomes target in Ukraine drone war
items.standfirst: Strikes on Wildberries have killed nine people and left small sellers facing millions of roubles in losses
items.content: Strikes on Wildberries have killed nine people and left small sellers facing millions of roubles in losses

items.title: Ukraine drone attack sparks fire at Russian e-commerce giant's warehouse near Saint Petersburg
items.standfirst: At least three people were wounded after a Ukrainian drone attack sparked a fire at a warehouse belonging to Russian e-commerce giant Wildberries near Saint Petersburg early Friday, regional officials said. Kyiv has struck five Wildberries warehouses since July 18, killing eight night-shift workers in a warehouse outside Moscow on Saturday and wounding dozens more.
items.content: At least three people were wounded after a Ukrainian drone attack sparked a fire at a warehouse belonging to Russian e-commerce giant Wildberries near Saint Petersburg early Friday, regional officials said. Kyiv has struck five Wildberries warehouses since July 18, killing eight night-shift workers in a warehouse outside Moscow on Saturday and wounding dozens more.

items.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
items.standfirst: Ukrainian drones have hit several retail warehouses belonging to Russia's biggest online retailer Wildberries in a matter of days.
items.content: Ukrainian drones have hit several retail warehouses belonging to Russia's biggest online retailer Wildberries in a matter of days.

items.content: The meeting comes hours after activist Sonam Wangchuk announced he was ending his 26-day hunger strike.

items.title: Indian activist Wangchuk ends 26-day hunger strike as 'Cockroach' protesters vow to press on
items.standfirst: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ended a 26-day hunger strike on Friday that helped galvanize a youth movement that's become one of India's largest protests in recent years, but demonstrators vowed to continue until the education minister resigns.
items.content: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ended a 26-day hunger strike on Friday that helped galvanize a youth movement that's become one of India's largest protests in recent years, but demonstrators vowed to continue until the education minister resigns.(Image credit: Vipin)

items.title: India ‘cockroach’ activist Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike as protests spread
items.standfirst: Sonam Wangchuk halted strike amid concerns about ‘possible violence’ in India as protest movement urges demonstrations across country on Friday over police brutalityThe Indian “cockroach” activist Sonam Wangchuk has ended a 26-day hunger strike over an exam paper scandal that has spiralled into a wide ranging, youth-led wave of demonstrations against Narendra Modi’s government.Supporters meanwhile have called a nationwide day of protest on Friday. Continue reading...
items.content: Sonam Wangchuk halted strike amid concerns about ‘possible violence’ in India as protest movement urges demonstrations across country on Friday over police brutalityThe Indian “cockroach” activist Sonam Wangchuk has ended a 26-day hunger strike over an exam paper scandal that has spiralled into a wide ranging, youth-led wave of demonstrations against Narendra Modi’s government.Supporters meanwhile have called a nationwide day of protest on Friday. Continue reading...

items.title: Indian activist ends 26-day hunger strike, though student protests continue
items.standfirst: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike on Friday after talks with government ministers, but student-led protests over leaked medical entrance exam papers continued, with demonstrators demanding the education minister's resignation and wider reforms.
items.content: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike on Friday after talks with government ministers, but student-led protests over leaked medical entrance exam papers continued, with demonstrators demanding the education minister's resignation and wider reforms.

items.title: ‘Cockroach’ protests: India’s Modi promises to punish officials over exam leaks
items.standfirst: PM says ‘those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared’ as scandal spirals into widespread angerNarendra Modi has vowed to set up “fast-track courts” to speed up the punishment of officials accused of leaking examination papers, as the Indian prime minister seeks to quell a youth-led “cockroach” protest movement that has rocked Delhi and posed one of the biggest challenges to his government.In his first public remarks on the movement, Modi posted on X: “Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth … Those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared.” Continue reading...
items.content: PM says ‘those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared’ as scandal spirals into widespread angerNarendra Modi has vowed to set up “fast-track courts” to speed up the punishment of officials accused of leaking examination papers, as the Indian prime minister seeks to quell a youth-led “cockroach” protest movement that has rocked Delhi and posed one of the biggest challenges to his government.In his first public remarks on the movement, Modi posted on X: “Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth … Those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared.” Continue reading...

Continue reading...
items.content: Evacuees have been told to leave using hundreds of mobilised boats or the only road linking the area with the mainlandin MadridOn Thursday night, authorities in the Madrid region asked the central government to step in and declare the fires around the region a national emergency. items.title: France orders total evacuation of Cap Ferret peninsula as wildfire spreads
items.standfirst: ‘Intense and highly unpredictable’ blaze forces more than 40,000 people to flee popular holiday spotEurope live – latest updatesAuthorities in south-western France have ordered the total evacuation of the Cap Ferret peninsula on the Atlantic coast by land and sea as an “intense and highly unpredictable” wildfire advances on one of the country’s best-known holiday spots.The fire had burned more than 10,000 hectares (24,700 acres) by Friday morning, the French interior minister, Laurent Nuñez, said, with 40,000 people already or about to be evacuated, including thousands of tourists from campsites and rental homes. Continue reading...
items.content: ‘Intense and highly unpredictable’ blaze forces more than 40,000 people to flee popular holiday spotEurope live – latest updatesAuthorities in south-western France have ordered the total evacuation of the Cap Ferret peninsula on the Atlantic coast by land and sea as an “intense and highly unpredictable” wildfire advances on one of the country’s best-known holiday spots.The fire had burned more than 10,000 hectares (24,700 acres) by Friday morning, the French interior minister, Laurent Nuñez, said, with 40,000 people already or about to be evacuated, including thousands of tourists from campsites and rental homes. Continue reading...

items.title: France orders total evacuation of Cap Ferret peninsula as wildfires sweep western Europe
items.standfirst: Wildfires fuelled by extreme heat and strong winds swept across western Europe on Friday, forcing French authorities to order the total evacuation of the Cap Ferret peninsula tourism hotspot. The blazes also triggered a state of emergency in Spain's Madrid region and prompted Paris to seek emergency assistance from the European Union.
items.content: Wildfires fuelled by extreme heat and strong winds swept across western Europe on Friday, forcing French authorities to order the total evacuation of the Cap Ferret peninsula tourism hotspot. The blazes also triggered a state of emergency in Spain's Madrid region and prompted Paris to seek emergency assistance from the European Union.

items.title: More than 15,000 people evacuated from homes as wildfire spreads in France
items.standfirst: Around 20,000 people were evacuated from towns and holiday resorts in southwestern France as a wildfire raged near the popular tourist coastal town of Lege Cap-Ferret.
items.content: Around 20,000 people were evacuated from towns and holiday resorts in southwestern France as a wildfire raged near the popular tourist coastal town of Lege Cap-Ferret.

items.title: New PM cuts business rates for UK pubs in latest cost-of-living measure
items.standfirst: British Prime Minister Andy Burnham announced a 20% cut to business rates for pubs, clubs and live music venues in England on Thursday, his third major cost-of-living measure in three days as the new Labour leader seeks to revive the economy and support struggling high streets.
items.content: British Prime Minister Andy Burnham announced a 20% cut to business rates for pubs, clubs and live music venues in England on Thursday, his third major cost-of-living measure in three days as the new Labour leader seeks to revive the economy and support struggling high streets.

items.title: UK ‘ready to defend itself’ after Iranian threat over US bases

clusters.title: Reinstating Fedorov 'only right decision Zelensky can make', MP says
clusters.neutral_headline: Ukraine's ousted defence minister insists on being reinstated

items.title: Reinstating Fedorov 'only right decision Zelensky can make', MP says
items.standfirst: Ousted Ukrainian defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov said on Thursday he would ​accept no other government position besides his former job, escalating a political crisis that has gripped Ukraine at a pivotal point in the war with Russia. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Ukranian MP Liudmyla Buimister says that reinstating Fedorov "is the only right decision that President Zelensky can make".
items.content: Ousted Ukrainian defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov said on Thursday he would ​accept no other government position besides his former job, escalating a political crisis that has gripped Ukraine at a pivotal point in the war with Russia. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Ukranian MP Liudmyla Buimister says that reinstating Fedorov "is the only right decision that President Zelensky can make".

items.title: Ukraine's ousted defence minister insists on being reinstated
items.standfirst: Mykhailo Fedorov refuses President Zelensky's offer to accept an alternative post, as the battle of wills continues.
items.content: Mykhailo Fedorov refuses President Zelensky's offer to accept an alternative post, as the battle of wills continues.
